title: odontites triboutii
intro: Odontites triboutii, also known as the French toothwort or Tribout's odontites, is a plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of hemiparasitic plant that obtains nutrients by connecting with the roots of other plants, typically grasses and sedges. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often prepared as a tincture or tea for its medicinal benefits, which are believed to include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and immune system support.
